Peter Paul Rubens

2 works in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
Samson and Delilah (Rubens)
by Peter Paul Rubens
Peter Paul Rubens · 1610 · National Gallery, London
Delilah watches tenderly while a Philistine crops Samson's hair as he sleeps on her lap. The candlelit nocturne and coiled composition mark Rubens's first mature statement after returning from Italy, still vibrating with Caravaggio.
The Garden of Love
by Peter Paul Rubens
Peter Paul Rubens · 1633 · Museo del Prado
A riotous outdoor party Rubens painted after his second marriage — his young wife Hélène Fourment appears three times among the guests. Cupids tug couples together while a fountain with the Three Graces soaks the scene in divinely sanctioned pleasure.
←Henri Rousseau Fariborz Sahba→
